title: solidago virgaurea subsp. turfosa
intro: Solidago virgaurea subsp. turfosa, also known as the Rough Goldenrod, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the aster family that is native to Europe and North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Solidago virgaurea subsp. turfosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, while also providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Solidago virgaurea subsp. turfosa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in salves and creams for topical application.
intro_study: According to "Biomolecules", Solidago virgaurea subsp. turfosa has anti-inflammatory benefits.
